title: Una Radoja
content:
People.ContactEmail uradoja@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2008
- University of British Columbia
Una is a leading commercial litigator and a nationally recognized environmental law expert.
Una Radoja*, a partner in McCarthy Tétrault's Litigation Group in Vancouver, has extensive experience successfully representing clients in high-stakes commercial disputes and complex environmental claims.
Una acts for clients across industries, including global metals and mining, real estate development, insurance, manufacturing, transportation and financial services.
Una is consistently ranked by Chambers Canada both in environmental law and general commercial litigation, by Lexpert as a leading practitioner in environmental law, and by Benchmark Litigation Canada as one of Canada’s Top 100 Women in Litigation.
Una represents clients in complex claims involving chemical spills and widespread multi-source contaminant migration, novel class actions concerning emerging contaminants, statutory and tort-based cost recovery claims and a variety of regulatory non-compliance matters.  She acts in judicial reviews before courts across Canada and appeals before tribunals from regulatory decisions involving environmental permits critical to projects.
In her advisory practice, Una provides strategic guidance on regulatory compliance, impact assessments, and environmental risk management.   She advises clients on environmental dimensions of high-value transactions, and guides them through sensitive compliance investigations, spill responses and all aspects of environmental risk management.

#### Commercial Litigation
Una is a distinguished and versatile commercial litigator with a reputation for excellence and exceptional client service.  Una acts for both plaintiffs and defendants in a variety of high-profile and multifaceted matters involving contractual claims, shareholder oppression claims, and claims for conspiracy, fraud and negligence arising from various commercial arrangements. Una has a specific interest in and a wealth of experience advancing and defending claims by public and private companies against directors and officers involving allegations of breaches of fiduciary duty, negligence, and IP theft.  Her broader practice encompasses infrastructure and engineering disputes, franchise disputes, and wrongful dismissal claims.
Una is a sought-after speaker and author. She regularly lectures at CBA CLEs, has co-authored British Columbia Environmental Management Legislation & Commentary , an essential guide to the regulation of BC’s contaminated sites, and is the editor of Halsbury’s Laws of Canada – Environment , a comprehensive resource for environmental law practitioners.
Una is deeply invested in supporting other lawyers and mentors many, both formally and informally. She serves as the national lead of McCarthy Tétrault’s Gender Equality Action Group and co-founded Life in Law, a non-profit society working to stem the exodus of women from the practice of law. She is involved in numerous industry associations and networks, including the Energy Storage Canada BC Policy Working Group, British Columbia Environment Industry Association, the Commercial Real Estate Women Network and NAIOP Vancouver Chapter.
*denotes Law Corporation
